title: UK Says No to Cigarettes for Kids Born After 2008
summary: The UK Parliament passed a new law that will stop children born after December 31, 2008 from ever buying cigarettes. This means that as these kids grow up, they will never be allowed to buy cigarettes, even when they become adults. The law also gives the government power to control how tobacco, vapes, and nicotine products are sold, including their flavors and packaging. Currently, it is illegal to sell cigarettes to people under 18, but this new law makes it so that the age to buy cigarettes goes up every year, so younger generations will never be old enough to buy them. The number of people who smoke in the UK has gone down a lot since the 1970s, but about 6.4 million people still smoke. Smoking causes about 80,000 deaths each year in the UK and is the number one cause of preventable death. The goal is to create a smoke-free generation and protect children from the harms of smoking.

keywords.explanation: The group of people who make laws in the UK.
Rolled tobacco that people smoke, which is very bad for health.
An official rule that says you cannot do something.

options: Flavors and packaging of tobacco and vapes
questions.correct_answer: Flavors and packaging of tobacco and vapes

background_read: Smoking is harmful because it can cause many diseases like cancer and heart problems. Many countries have laws to stop young people from smoking. The UK wants to make sure that children born after 2008 never start smoking.
Article_Structure: WHO: The UK Parliament and children born after 2008.
WHAT: A new law banning cigarette purchases for future generations.
WHY: To reduce smoking, prevent deaths, and protect children's health.

perspectives.description: They support the ban because smoking causes many deaths and diseases.
They might feel the ban is unfair because it limits their freedom.
